title: Avoid clear-text communication protocols
description: Using clear-text protocols like HTTP, FTP, or Telnet exposes data to eavesdropping and man-in-the-middle attacks. Sensitive information transmitted over unencrypted channels can be intercepted and compromised.
recommendation: Use encrypted protocols like HTTPS, SFTP, or SSH instead of their clear-text equivalents.

noncompliant.description: Using HTTP to transmit sensitive data exposes it to eavesdropping.
Using FTP exposes credentials and data in clear text.

compliant.description: Use HTTPS to ensure encrypted communication for sensitive data.
Use SSH/SFTP for secure file transfer operations.

checks.message: Use encrypted protocols instead of clear text
